title: Wave-0.5: enforce tier gate on the active-test (DAST) surface per business.md

files_to_modify: messages/en.json (+ hi/ml/es/de) ONLY if a new upgrade-prompt string is required

acceptance_criteria: Read .claude/project/business.md (active DAST unlocked by higher tiers; tier enforcement is code-level via hasTier/tier-gating.ts) and src/lib/tier-gating.ts + src/lib/auth/helpers.ts (hasTier hierarchy free<one-shot<pro<studio).

Enforce the tier gate at BOTH layers: (1) the /active-test page/flow must NOT render the DAST wizard for a user below the required tier — show the existing upgrade prompt/CTA (use tier-gating.ts getUpgradeMessage / canAccessFeature; reuse existing upgrade UX, do not invent a new design); (2) the POST /api/v1/active-test/start route must reject a below-tier user server-side (the authoritative gate) BEFORE any scan work, returning a clear non-200 (consistent with the route's existing error shape) — defense in depth, the UI gate alone is insufficient.

Determine the required minimum tier from business.md + tier-gating.ts (the paid DAST surface starts at 'one-shot'); free is blocked, one-shot/pro/studio allowed. Do NOT hard-code outside tier-gating.ts's hierarchy — use hasTier/the existing helper.
The existing auth + domain-verification gates remain unchanged and still apply (this ADDS the missing tier gate; it does not replace the others).
Behaviour-preserving for entitled users: a one-shot+ user with a verified domain still completes the DAST flow exactly as before.
If a new user-facing string is needed, add it to ALL message catalogs (en/hi/ml/es/de) per the i18n convention.

npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm run test, npm run build all pass. Surface in the report exactly which tier is required and the file:line of both enforcement points (for Phase-4 review and so T-10 can assert the real behaviour).
output_format: code plus plain English: required tier, both enforcement points (file:line), and confirmation entitled-user behaviour is unchanged
